fullName: Electricity Safety, Quality and Continuity Regulations 2002
statusDate: In force; amended most recently by SI 2009/639
authority: Secondary legislation made under the Electricity Act 1989
summary: Statutory safety rules for generators, network operators, and meter operators. Sets mandatory requirements for the protection of persons and property from electrical hazards, the quality of supply (voltage/frequency), and continuity of supply. Enforced by the Health and Safety Executive (HSE) via the Department for Business and Trade.
scope: Duties on generators, TOs, DNOs, IDNOs, and meter operators
Protection of the public from electrical hazards
Statutory supply voltage tolerances (230 V ±10%, 400 V 3-phase)
Duty to investigate and report interruptions, overvoltage events, and fatalities
Minimum clearances for overhead lines, cables, and substations

keyParts.name: Part II — General Provisions
keyParts.detail: Safety duties of generators and distributors, protection equipment, earthing.

keyParts.name: Part III — Generation
keyParts.detail: Requirements specific to generators including embedded generation.

keyParts.name: Part IV — Distribution and Transmission
keyParts.detail: Overhead line clearances, underground cables, substations, voltage and frequency tolerances.

keyParts.name: Part V — Supply to Consumers' Installations
keyParts.detail: Connection of consumer installations; the interface between the distributor and the consumer.

notableSections.name: Generators
notableSections.detail: Generator's installation must not cause danger, interference with supply quality, or damage to equipment of others.

notableSections.name: Generation set to be connected in parallel
notableSections.detail: Fundamental basis for G98/G99 protection settings.

notableSections.name: Notification of fatal and non-fatal accidents
notableSections.detail: Mandatory reporting to the Secretary of State.
